---
title: "Who owns India's plant varieties?"
subtitle: "Explore the PPV&FRA certification data"
---

```{r setup}
source("R/prep_dashboard.R")

trends_df <- chr(sel_for(dd) %>% filter(!is.na(sector)) %>%
  count(sel, issue_year, sector, name = "n"))

comp_all <- d %>% filter(!is.na(sector)) %>% count(sector, name = "n") %>% mutate(sel = "All crops")
comp_df  <- chr(bind_rows(comp_all, sel_for(d) %>% filter(!is.na(sector)) %>% count(sel, sector, name = "n")))

firm_key    <- sel_for(d) %>% filter(applicant_category == "Private") %>%
  mutate(firm = alias_company(canon_company(applicant)))
firm_labels <- firm_key %>% count(firm, applicant) %>% group_by(firm) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>% ungroup() %>%
  transmute(firm, label = coalesce(unname(pretty_names[firm]), applicant))
firm_df <- chr(firm_key %>% count(sel, firm, name = "n") %>%
  group_by(sel) %>% slice_max(n, n = 10, with_ties = FALSE) %>% ungroup() %>%
  left_join(firm_labels, by = "firm"))

var_df <- chr(sel_for(d) %>% filter(!is.na(variety_category), !is.na(sector)) %>%
  count(sel, sector, variety_category, name = "n"))

cum_sel <- sel_for(dd) %>% filter(!is.na(sector)) %>% count(sel, issue_year, sector, name = "n")
cum_all <- dd %>% filter(!is.na(sector)) %>% count(issue_year, sector, name = "n") %>%
  mutate(sel = "All crops")
cum_df  <- chr(bind_rows(cum_all, cum_sel) %>% arrange(sel, sector, issue_year) %>%
  group_by(sel, sector) %>% mutate(cum = cumsum(n)) %>% ungroup())

ojs_define(categories_ojs = crop_categories)
ojs_define(crops_ojs      = top_crops)
ojs_define(trends_ojs = trends_df)
ojs_define(comp_ojs   = comp_df)
ojs_define(firm_ojs   = firm_df)
ojs_define(var_ojs    = var_df)
ojs_define(cum_ojs    = cum_df)
```

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**`r format(n_total, big.mark=",")` certificates** analysed, from the
[PPV&FR Authority register](https://plantauthority.gov.in/list-certificates).
Last data refresh: **`r last_updated`**. This page rebuilds every week.
:::

Every chart below answers the same set of questions for whichever crop you pick: who holds
certificates, is the market concentrated, and how has that changed over time. Certificates
are filed by five applicant types: Farmer, Private, Public, SAU (State Agricultural
Universities), and Individual Breeder. For the full write-up, background, and the cotton
case study, see [Full analysis](analysis.qmd).

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple"}
**These charts are interactive.** Each one has two dropdowns: a crop category (like
Cereals) and a specific crop (like Brinjal). Pick one and the other greys out, so it's
always clear which selection is driving the chart.
:::

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
categories = categories_ojs
crops = crops_ojs
sectorDomain = ["Farmer", "Private", "Public", "SAU", "Individual Breeder"]
sectorRange  = ["#4daf4a", "#377eb8", "#e41a1c", "#ff7f00", "#984ea3"]
catDomain = ["Farmer", "New", "Extant", "Extant (Notified)", "Extant (VCK)", "EDV"]
catRange  = ["#4daf4a", "#377eb8", "#80b1d3", "#ff7f00", "#e41a1c", "#984ea3"]

// Custom paired viewof: a category dropdown and a crop dropdown side by side.
// Whichever one you touch becomes active; the other disables and greys out
// so there's no ambiguity about which selection the chart is using.
function pairedCropInput({catOptions = categories, cropOptions = crops,
                           catValue, cropValue, catLabel, cropLabel,
                           defaultMode = "crop"} = {}) {
  const catSel  = Inputs.select(catOptions,  {label: catLabel  ?? "Crop category", value: catValue  ?? catOptions[0]});
  const cropSel = Inputs.select(cropOptions, {label: cropLabel ?? "Specific crop", value: cropValue ?? cropOptions[0]});
  const box = html`<div style="display:flex;gap:2rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:flex-end;margin-bottom:0.5rem">
    <div>${catSel}</div><div>${cropSel}</div></div>`;

  // Both dropdowns stay fully clickable at all times. "Disabled" here is
  // purely visual (dimmed) so the inactive one can still be clicked to
  // switch modes back — a truly HTML-disabled <select> can't be re-enabled
  // by clicking it, which is why that approach doesn't work here.
  function setMode(mode) {
    const catActive = mode === "category";
    catSel.style.opacity  = catActive ? "1" : "0.45";
    cropSel.style.opacity = catActive ? "0.45" : "1";
    box.value = catActive ? catSel.value : cropSel.value;
    box.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", {bubbles: true}));
  }

  catSel.addEventListener("input",  (e) => { e.stopPropagation(); setMode("category"); });
  cropSel.addEventListener("input", (e) => { e.stopPropagation(); setMode("crop"); });
  setMode(defaultMode === "crop" ? "crop" : "category");
  return box;
}
```

## Certificate trends by crop

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sel1 = pairedCropInput({catValue: "Cereals", cropValue: "Cotton"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 55,
  marginBottom: 55,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Year", tickFormat: "d", tickRotate: -45},
  y: {label: "Certificates", grid: true},
  color: {legend: true, domain: sectorDomain, range: sectorRange},
  marks: [
    Plot.barY(transpose(trends_ojs).filter(d => d.sel === sel1),
      {x: "issue_year", y: "n", fill: "sector", tip: true}),
    Plot.ruleY([0])
  ]
})
```

Annual certificates for the selected crop, stacked by applicant category.

## Applicant composition by crop

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sel2 = pairedCropInput({catValue: "Cereals", cropValue: "Cotton"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof mode2 = Inputs.radio(["Percentage", "Counts"], {label: "Show", value: "Percentage"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
rows2 = {
  const all = transpose(comp_ojs).filter(d => d.sel === "All crops").map(d => ({...d, group: "All crops"}));
  const sel = transpose(comp_ojs).filter(d => d.sel === sel2).map(d => ({...d, group: sel2}));
  return all.concat(sel);
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 90,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: mode2 === "Percentage" ? "Share of certificates" : "Certificates",
      percent: mode2 === "Percentage", grid: true},
  y: {label: null},
  color: {legend: true, domain: sectorDomain, range: sectorRange},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(rows2, {x: "n", y: "group", fill: "sector",
      offset: mode2 === "Percentage" ? "normalize" : null, tip: true}),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})
```

Two thin bars: all crops on top, your selection below. Switch to Percentage to compare
shape rather than scale.

## Firm-level concentration

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sel3 = pairedCropInput({catValue: "Cereals", cropValue: "Cotton"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 220,
  marginRight: 20,
  width: 760,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Certificates", grid: true},
  y: {label: null},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(transpose(firm_ojs).filter(d => d.sel === sel3),
      {x: "n", y: "label", sort: {y: "x", reverse: true}, fill: "#377eb8", tip: true}),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})
```

The top ten private applicants for the selected crop.

## Variety category breakdown

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sel4 = pairedCropInput({catValue: "Cereals", cropValue: "Cotton"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sec4 = Inputs.select(["All"].concat(sectorDomain), {label: "Applicant", value: "Private"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
rows4 = {
  let r = transpose(var_ojs).filter(d => d.sel === sel4);
  if (sec4 !== "All") r = r.filter(d => d.sector === sec4);
  return r;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 55,
  marginBottom: 80,
  style: {fontSize: "14px"},
  x: {label: null, tickRotate: -30},
  y: {label: "Certificates", grid: true},
  color: {legend: true, domain: catDomain, range: catRange},
  marks: [
    Plot.barY(rows4, {x: "variety_category", y: "n", fill: "variety_category", tip: true}),
    Plot.ruleY([0])
  ]
})
```

Certificates split across variety categories for the selected crop and applicant. Try
Private together with Cotton.

## Cumulative ownership over time

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sel5 = pairedCropInput({
  catOptions: ["All crops"].concat(categories), catValue: "All crops",
  cropValue: "Cotton", defaultMode: "category"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof yr5 = Inputs.range(
  [2009, d3.max(transpose(cum_ojs), d => d.issue_year)],
  {step: 1, label: "Up to year",
   value: d3.max(transpose(cum_ojs), d => d.issue_year)})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
rows5 = transpose(cum_ojs).filter(d => d.sel === sel5 && d.issue_year <= yr5)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 55,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Year", tickFormat: "d"},
  y: {label: "Cumulative certificates", grid: true},
  color: {legend: true, domain: sectorDomain, range: sectorRange},
  marks: [
    Plot.line(rows5, {x: "issue_year", y: "cum", stroke: "sector", z: "sector"}),
    Plot.dot(rows5, {x: "issue_year", y: "cum", fill: "sector", r: 3, tip: true}),
    Plot.ruleY([0])
  ]
})
```

Drag the year slider to watch ownership build up rather than just seeing the endpoint.

---

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
For the headline findings, the cotton case study, and full methodology, see
[Full analysis](analysis.qmd). Pipeline source: [project repository](https://github.com/ambika-subash/pvp-certification-explorer).
:::
